event: Johannes Gutenberg invents the printing press.
Zacharias Janssen invents the compound microscope.
Hans Lippershey invents the telescope.
Cornelis Drebbel invents the first navigable submarine.
Blaise Pascal invents the mechanical calculator.
Evangelista Torricelli invents the barometer.
Otto von Guericke invents the air pump.
Christiaan Huygens invents the pendulum clock.
Thomas Savery patents the first crude steam engine.
Daniel Gabriel Fahrenheit invents the alcohol thermometer.
Thomas Newcomen builds the first practical steam engine.
Daniel Gabriel Fahrenheit invents the mercury thermometer.
John Kay invents the flying shuttle.
Benjamin Franklin invents the lightning rod.
James Hargreaves invents the spinning jenny.
James Watt patents his improved steam engine.
David Bushnell invents the Turtle, a submersible.
The Montgolfier brothers launch the first hot air balloon.
Edmund Cartwright invents the power loom.
Eli Whitney invents the cotton gin.
Alessandro Volta invents the voltaic pile (battery).
Richard Trevithick builds the first full-scale working railway steam locomotive.
Robert Fulton builds the first commercially successful steamboat.
George Stephenson builds the first practical steam locomotive.
Nicéphore Niépce takes the first permanent photograph.
Michael Faraday discovers electromagnetic induction.
Charles Babbage designs the Analytical Engine.
Samuel Morse patents the telegraph.
Charles Goodyear invents vulcanized rubber.
Samuel Morse sends the first public telegraph message.
Elias Howe patents the sewing machine.
Elisha Otis invents the safety elevator.
The Bessemer process for steel production is patented.
Gaston Planté invents the lead-acid battery.
Richard Gatling patents the Gatling gun.
Alfred Nobel invents dynamite.
Christopher Latham Sholes patents the modern typewriter.
Alexander Graham Bell patents the telephone.
Thomas Edison invents the phonograph.
Thomas Edison patents a commercially practical incandescent light bulb.
Hiram Maxim invents the first fully automatic machine gun.
Karl Benz builds the first practical automobile powered by an internal combustion engine.
Nikola Tesla patents the alternating current (AC) induction motor.
Wilhelm Röntgen discovers X-rays.
Guglielmo Marconi patents a wireless telegraph system.
Guglielmo Marconi successfully transmits a radio signal across the Atlantic.
The Wright brothers achieve the first powered, controlled, and sustained heavier-than-air flight.
John Ambrose Fleming invents the vacuum tube diode.
Lee de Forest invents the triode vacuum tube.
Henry Ford introduces the Model T automobile.
Henry Ford introduces the first moving assembly line for cars.
The first commercial radio broadcast is made by KDKA in Pittsburgh.
Robert Goddard launches the first liquid-fueled rocket.
Philo Farnsworth transmits the first all-electronic television image.
Alexander Fleming discovers penicillin.
Frank Whittle patents the jet engine.
Robert Watson-Watt patents radar.
Chester Carlson invents the photocopier (xerography).
Igor Sikorsky develops the first successful helicopter.
Enrico Fermi oversees the first controlled nuclear chain reaction.
The first atomic bomb is detonated (Trinity test).
ENIAC, the first general-purpose electronic digital computer, is dedicated.
John Bardeen, Walter Brattain, and William Shockley invent the transistor.
UNIVAC I, the first commercial computer, is delivered to the US Census Bureau.
The first nuclear power plant to generate electricity for a power grid starts operation in Obninsk.
Jonas Salk's polio vaccine is declared safe and effective.
The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, the first artificial Earth satellite.
Jack Kilby and Robert Noyce independently invent the integrated circuit.
Theodore Maiman builds the first working laser.
Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human in space.
IBM announces the System/360 family of mainframe computers.
Douglas Engelbart demonstrates the computer mouse and graphical user interface.
ARPANET, the precursor to the Internet, is established.
Intel introduces the 4004, the first commercial microprocessor.
Martin Cooper makes the first call on a portable cellular phone.
The Altair 8800, sparking the microcomputer revolution, is released.
Apple Computer is founded by Steve Jobs and Steve Wozniak.
Sony introduces the Walkman portable cassette player.
IBM introduces the IBM Personal Computer (PC).
The first commercial compact disc (CD) is produced.
The ARPANET transitions to TCP/IP, marking the birth of the modern Internet.
Apple introduces the Macintosh computer.
Tim Berners-Lee proposes the World Wide Web.
The first web server and web browser are created.
Linus Torvalds releases the first version of the Linux kernel.
The Mosaic web browser is released, popularizing the World Wide Web.
The first smartphone, the IBM Simon, is released.
The DVD format is announced.
Dolly the sheep becomes the first mammal cloned from an adult somatic cell.
IBM's Deep Blue defeats world chess champion Garry Kasparov.
